subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the literal meaning and the story behind the poem
Identify the child's feelings of hope and imagination
Learn the sequence of the child’s actions (writing names, loading flowers)
Recognize basic rhyming words and the rhythm of the poem
Develop a connection between personal play and poetic expression
Identify the 'Shiuli' flowers and their significance in the poem

lesson_plan.title: Floating the Boats
topics_covered: Introduction to the poet, Rabindranath Tagore
The child's action of writing names on boats
teaching_strategy: Warm-up: 'Have you ever made a paper boat? Where did you float it?'
Guided reading of the first few lines
Discussion: Why does the child write his name and village on the boats?
student_activities: Draw a picture of a paper boat on a stream
Write their own name and village 'in big black letters' as described in the poem

lesson_plan.title: Special Cargo: The Shiuli Flowers
topics_covered: Loading the boats with Shiuli flowers
The child's hope: 'Land safely in the night'
teaching_strategy: Show a picture of Shiuli (Night-flowering jasmine) flowers
Explain the metaphor of 'blooms of the dawn'
Read aloud with emphasis on the hopeful tone of the child
student_activities: List the things the child puts in his boats
Vocabulary match: Match words like 'Stream', 'Laden', and 'Dawn' with simple pictures

lesson_plan.title: Dreaming of the Journey
topics_covered: Looking up at the sky: The clouds as 'white bulging sails'
The child's dream of a playmate in the sky
Who is 'launching' the boats in the clouds?
teaching_strategy: Discussion on imagination: Seeing clouds as boats
Identifying the 'fairies of sleep' and the 'baskets full of dreams'
Explaining the simple rhyme and rhythm of the verses
student_activities: Coloring activity: Clouds that look like sails
Write 2 simple sentences about where they want their paper boat to go

lesson_plan.title: Recitation and Craft
teaching_strategy: Model recitation with soft, flowing movements (like a boat)
Choral recitation: The whole class reciting together
Hands-on activity: Making a paper boat
student_activities: Paper folding: Making a real paper boat
Recite their favorite stanza from the poem
assessment: Completion of the paper boat craft

teaching_aids: Images of Rabindranath Tagore and Shiuli flowers
A bowl of water (optional) for a live demonstration
evaluation_methods: Sequence ordering (What did the child do first, second...)
teacher_tip: Focus on the 'wonder' of childhood. Don't worry too much about deep metaphors yet; just help them feel the child's excitement as he sends his name out into the world. Use the paper boat making activity as a reward at the end!
